title: Reference
text:
Set up project infrastructure in the specified directory.
Usage: infra setup [OPTIONS] [DIRECTORY]

Options:
  --type TYPE         Project type: single or compendium (default: single)
  --image TYPE        Devcontainer image type: bioconductor, rocker, or python
                      (default: bioconductor)
  --add-gha           Add prebuild-devcontainer GitHub Actions workflow
  --install-repos     Install the MiguelRodo/repos utility
  --no-readme         Skip creating README.md
  --no-repos-list     Skip creating repos.list
  --force             Overwrite existing files
  --name NAME         Project name for README (default: directory basename)
  --prebuild-tag TAG  Pre-built image tag to use (default: fetch latest from GitHub)
  -h, --help          Show this help message


# Set up current directory with defaults (BioConductor devcontainer, single repo)
infra setup .

# Compendium with Python devcontainer and GHA prebuild workflow
infra setup --type compendium --image python --add-gha .

# Rocker verse devcontainer, also install repos utility
infra setup --image rocker --install-repos ~/my-project

# Overwrite existing files
infra setup --force .










File
Description




README.md
Project README with aim, contact, links, details sections


repos.list
Repository list for use with repos clone


.devcontainer/devcontainer.json
Build-based devcontainer (uses local Dockerfile)


.devcontainer/Dockerfile
Dockerfile for the chosen image type


.devcontainer/prebuild/devcontainer.json
Pre-built image devcontainer


.devcontainer/renv/.gitkeep
renv cache placeholder (R images only)


.github/workflows/prebuild-devcontainer.yml
GHA workflow (if --add-gha specified)















Type
Base image
R support
renv cache




bioconductor
bioconductor/bioconductor_docker:RELEASE_3_21-r-4.5.2
✓
✓


rocker
rocker/verse:latest
✓
✓


python
python:3.12-slim
✗
✗

title: Reference
text:
infra install-repos
Install the MiguelRodo/repos multi-repository management utility in an OS-appropriate manner.
Usage: infra install-repos [OPTIONS]

Options:
  --force     Re-install even if already installed
  -h, --help  Show this help message

Examples
infra install-repos
infra install-repos --force

title: Reference
text:
Global options
infra --help      Show help
infra --version   Show version
infra -h          Alias for --help
infra -V          Alias for --version

title: Installation
text: You can install infra as a system package (Ubuntu/Debian, macOS, Windows), from source, or as a language-specific wrapper (R or Python).

title: Installation
text:
Ubuntu / Debian

Option 1: Install from APT Repository (Recommended)
Install and keep infra up to date directly via apt:
# Add repository signing key
curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/MiguelRodo/apt-miguelrodo/main/KEY.gpg \
   | sudo gpg --dearmor -o /usr/share/keyrings/miguelrodo-infra.gpg

# Add apt source
echo "deb [signed-by=/usr/share/keyrings/miguelrodo-infra.gpg] https://raw.githubusercontent.com/MiguelRodo/apt-miguelrodo/main/ ./" \
   | sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/miguelrodo-infra.list >/dev/null

# Install infra
sudo apt-get update
sudo apt-get install -y infra
To uninstall:
sudo apt-get remove infra


Option 2: Local Installation (No sudo required)
Install to your user directory (~/.local/bin):
git clone https://github.com/MiguelRodo/infra.git
cd infra
bash install-local.sh
If ~/.local/bin is not on your PATH, add this to ~/.bashrc or ~/.profile:
export PATH="$HOME/.local/bin:$PATH"
Then reload:
source ~/.bashrc
To uninstall:
bash uninstall-local.sh


Option 3: System-wide from Release .deb (requires sudo)
Download and install the latest .deb from the Releases page:
VERSION_INFRA=1.0.0
wget https://github.com/MiguelRodo/infra/releases/download/v${VERSION_INFRA}/infra_${VERSION_INFRA}_all.deb
sudo dpkg -i infra_${VERSION_INFRA}_all.deb
rm infra_${VERSION_INFRA}_all.deb

# Fix any dependency issues
sudo apt-get install -f
To uninstall:
sudo dpkg -r infra


Dependencies
All Ubuntu/Debian methods require bash, curl, and jq. These are typically pre-installed. If not:
sudo apt-get install bash curl jq

title: Installation
text:
macOS (Homebrew)
brew tap MiguelRodo/infra
brew install infra
The formula automatically handles the jq dependency.

title: Installation
text:
Windows

Scoop (recommended)
scoop bucket add infra https://github.com/MiguelRodo/scoop-bucket
scoop install infra
git and jq are installed automatically by Scoop. You will also need Git for Windows for Bash support.


Manual

Clone the repository:
git clone https://github.com/MiguelRodo/infra.git
cd infra
Run the installer:
.\install.ps1
Restart your PowerShell session.

Dependencies:

Git for Windows — provides bash, git, and curl
jq — JSON processor

title: Installation
text:
From Source
git clone https://github.com/MiguelRodo/infra.git
cd infra

# Local install (no sudo)
bash install-local.sh

# Or system-wide on Ubuntu/Debian
sudo dpkg-buildpackage -us -uc -b
sudo dpkg -i ../infra_*.deb

title: Installation
text:
Language Wrappers

R Package
install.packages("devtools")
devtools::install_github("MiguelRodo/infra")
Requires bash, curl, and jq on your system.


Python Package
pip install git+https://github.com/MiguelRodo/infra.git
Or from a local clone:
git clone https://github.com/MiguelRodo/infra.git
cd infra
pip install .
Requires bash, curl, and jq on your system. On Windows, use Git for Windows or WSL.

title: infra
text: Set up standard project infrastructure — devcontainers, README templates, and repository lists — for both individual repositories and multi-repo compendia, with a single command.

title: infra
text:
Quick Start
# Set up the current directory with defaults (BioConductor devcontainer)
infra setup .

# Python devcontainer with a GHA prebuild workflow
infra setup --image python --add-gha .

# Multi-repo compendium with Rocker devcontainer, also install repos utility
infra setup --type compendium --image rocker --install-repos .

text:

.devcontainer/renv/.gitkeep
renv cache placeholder (R images only)


.github/workflows/prebuild-devcontainer.yml
GHA workflow (if --add-gha specified)
